purpose: Cross-document argument-consistency map: given an author's corpus of argument-shaped pieces (a FOCAL document vs the rest of a supplied pool), produces a DESCRIPTIVE map of where the corpus's load-bearing commitments are in tension across documents — a claim asserted in A that a claim in B undercuts, a scope condition honored in A but dropped in B, a value premise that flips. The tensions ARE the read — NEVER a single "consistency score", NEVER a verdict about the author. The argument-CONTENT sibling of cross_doc_novelty_profile (the stylometric sibling); the nonfiction-argument analogue of Series Continuity / world-bible self-consistency. The no-verdict firewall is MECHANICAL, not rhetorical: a FORBIDDEN_RESULT_KEYS frozenset (hypocrisy / dishonest / bad_faith / contradicts_self / who_is_right / author_verdict / winning_document / consistency_score / author_score) + a FORBIDDEN_SUBSTRINGS tuple + a recursive assert_no_verdict() guard (raises ConsistencyVerdictError) called immediately before build_output, routing to available:false / policy_refused. The legitimate-variation FILTER is a required mechanical stage: every surface tension is run through five defenses in a fixed precedence (retraction -> time -> scope -> audience -> genre); the first defense whose REQUIRED textual evidence is present fires -> defended_<that>; none -> genuine. Defended tensions APPEAR in the ledger marked defended_*. Filter-integrity is mechanical: a defended_* tension with an empty rationale is a BUILD ERROR. Commitment extraction is a NEW argument_judge-style LLM-judge pass over free text (extract_commitments), NOT argument_spine parsing. M1 = mock-deterministic judge (CI-safe); M2 = anthropic (lazy-import / fail-loud). Ships calibration_status: heuristic — directional, NO numeric anchor (no measured discrimination).
use_when: You have an author's corpus of two or more argument-shaped documents (op-eds, policy briefs, essays, testimony, white papers) and want a descriptive map of where their load-bearing commitments are in tension across documents, with verbatim loci and a named class of resolution

You want defended variations (a position that evolved over time, narrowed in scope, was calibrated to a different audience or register, or was explicitly retracted) shown AS defended, not hidden and not scored as inconsistency
You need a CI-runnable M1 read with a mock-deterministic judge (no API dependency); the M2 anthropic judge is the real extraction lens
do_not_use_when: You want a verdict on the author — a finding of hypocrisy, dishonesty, bad faith, or self-contradiction; the surface refuses this by construction (the no-verdict firewall raises)
You want a single "consistency score", an author score, or a "winning document" selection — there is no top-level score and the surface picks no winner
You want adjudication of which document is correct, or a rewrite of the arguments — the surface names classes of resolution only, it does not adjudicate or rewrite
You have a single document (this is a CROSS-document surface; use within_doc_segmentation for a single text), or the documents are below the length floor (default 50 words each)

inputs.focal: a --focal path (the document compared against the rest of the pool; required)
inputs.pool: a --reference-manifest (JSONL; id + text|text_path) OR a --reference-dir of texts (.txt/.md, recursive)
inputs.knobs: --judge mock (M1, deterministic CI) | manifest | anthropic/openai/gemini (M2, fail-loud), --judge-model (for an API judge), --judge-manifest, --max-docs (default 5), --length-floor-words (default 50)

compute.cost_note: M1 mock-deterministic judge is pure-stdlib (marker-driven extraction + deterministic relation + a mechanical keyword-evidence legitimate-variation filter); no models, no API. The M2 anthropic/openai/gemini judge is a lazy-import / fail-loud seam (one API call per document).
